item.title: LongLive-RAG: A General Retrieval-Augmented Framework for Long Video Generation
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Autoregressive (AR) video diffusion enables variable-length synthesis, but long-horizon generation often suffers from accumulated errors and identity drift. For efficiency, existing methods commonly adopt sliding-window attention during generation. This creates an irreversible generation trajecto...

item.title: MCP-Persona: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Real-World Personal Applications via Environment Simulation
item.description: 📄 New in cs.AI — The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has emerged as a transformative standard for connecting large language models (LLMs) with external data sources and tools, and has been rapidly adopted across personal applications and development platforms. However, existing benchmarks predominantly focus on gene...

item.title: RoboSemanticBench: Diagnosing Semantic Grounding in Action Prediction for VLA Models
item.description: 📄 New in cs.RO — Vision-language-action (VLA) models are built on the premise that semantic understanding from pretrained language or vision-language backbones should guide robot action prediction. Yet robot fine-tuning is optimized as imitation over task-specific action distributions, and many evaluations can be...

item.title: VLMs are Good Teachers for Video Reasoning via Adaptive Test-Time Optimization
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — The recent "Reasoning with Video" paradigm utilizes Video Generation Models (VGMs) to generate temporally coherent visual trajectories to complete reasoning tasks. Although state-of-the-art VGMs excel at visual quality, they often struggle to understand and follow task-specific rules, leading to ...

item.title: On the Scaling of PEFT: Towards Million Personal Models of Trillion Parameters
item.description: 📄 New in cs.LG, cs.CL — Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) is usually treated as a cheaper alternative to full fine-tuning. We study a broader role: small trainable adapters as persistent local state on top of strong shared foundation models. In this framing, the base model provides shared competence while adapters ...

item.title: Multi-Agent Computer Use
item.description: 📄 New in cs.MA, cs.CL — Computer use agents (CUAs) today are primarily deployed as single serial agents. This setup is suboptimal for complex long-horizon tasks that benefit from task decomposition, parallel execution, and consistent re-planning based on new information. In this paper, we argue that we should instead mo...

item.title: Thinking in Blender: Staged Executable Inverse Graphics with Vision-Language Models
item.description: 📄 New in cs.CV — Inverse graphics is a longstanding and highly underconstrained problem that seeks to reconstruct images as editable 3D scenes which can be rendered, relit, and manipulated. In this work, we investigate whether pretrained vision-language models (VLMs) can perform executable inverse graphics direct...

item.title: anthropics / claude-code
item.description: ⭐ 599 GitHub stars today · ⭐ 129.5k total stars — Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal, understands your codebase, and helps you code faster by executing routine tasks, explaining complex code, and handling git workflows - all through natural language commands.

item.title: harry0703 / MoneyPrinterTurbo
item.description: ⭐ 3.4k GitHub stars today · ⭐ 77.6k total stars — 利用AI大模型，一键生成高清短视频 Generate short videos with one click using AI LLM.

item.title: HandsOnLLM / Hands-On-Large-Language-Models
item.description: ⭐ 36 stars today on GitHub · ⭐ 26.7k total stars — Official code repo for the O'Reilly Book - "Hands-On Large Language Models"
